weapons.name: Daemonic (3)
weapons.range: TB 3 against all damage (included in armour). Immune to poison, disease, and environmental hazards. Unstable — takes 1d10 damage per round from Holy attacks or blessed weapons.

weapons.name: Fear (2)
weapons.range: All characters within sight must pass a Difficult (-10) Willpower test or suffer Fear effects.

weapons.name: Toxic (2)
weapons.range: Any creature hit by this daemon's Plague Sword must pass a Challenging (+0) Toughness test or suffer 2d10 additional damage (ignores armour and Toughness Bonus).

weapons.name: Cloud of Flies
weapons.range: All creatures within 3m suffer -10 to WS, BS, and Perception tests.

weapons.name: From Beyond
weapons.range: Immune to Fear, Pinning, Insanity, and mind-affecting powers.

weapons.name: Warp Instability
weapons.range: If the Plaguebearer takes damage and fails a Willpower test, it takes additional damage equal to its degrees of failure. If reduced to 0 wounds, it is banished to the Warp.

system.skills:
S: | T: | Ag:
Int: Forbidden Lore (Daemonology)
Per: Awareness +10
WP:
Fel:

description.value:
Plaguebearers are the Lesser Daemons of Nurgle, the Plague God. They are shambling, rotting humanoids with a single rheumy eye, cracked antlers, and distended bellies filled with pestilence. They count the diseases of the universe in an endless, droning tally.

Tactics: Plaguebearers advance slowly and relentlessly. They do not dodge or seek cover — they simply walk forward, absorbing damage with their unnatural resilience, and strike with their rusted Plague Swords. Their Cloud of Flies debuffs nearby enemies. They are extremely durable and must be destroyed through sustained, focused fire. Holy or blessed weapons are dramatically more effective.
